evals.name: composable-creation
evals.prompt: I need a composable that tracks whether the user has scrolled past a certain threshold. It should return a reactive boolean.

assertions.text: Creates a function named useScrollThreshold or similar with use prefix
Uses ref() for the boolean state, not reactive()
Registers scroll listener in onMounted() (SSR-safe)
Removes scroll listener in onUnmounted() (cleanup)
Returns a plain object with the reactive ref
Places file in src/composables/

evals.name: ref-vs-reactive-decision
evals.prompt: Should I use reactive() for my form state object that has email, password, and remember fields?

assertions.text: States that this project uses ref() exclusively, never reactive()
Shows the pattern: ref({ email: '', password: '', remember: false })
Explains that ref allows reassignment and consistent .value access
Warns against destructuring ref.value (loses reactivity)

evals.name: ssr-safe-pattern
evals.prompt: I want to read the user's preferred color scheme from localStorage when the component loads. How do I do this safely with SSR?

assertions.text: Wraps localStorage access in onMounted() or typeof window check
Explains that setup() runs on both server and client
Shows code with onMounted(() => { localStorage.getItem(...) })
Never accesses localStorage at the top level of setup

evals.name: lifecycle-hooks-usage
evals.prompt: I need to set up a WebSocket connection when my component mounts and close it when the component is destroyed.

assertions.text: Uses onMounted() to establish the WebSocket connection
Uses onUnmounted() to close the connection (cleanup)
Stores the socket reference in a variable accessible to both hooks
Does NOT use onBeforeMount or setup-level connection (SSR unsafe)

evals.name: watcher-setup
evals.prompt: I want to fetch new data whenever the route parameter 'id' changes. What's the correct pattern?

assertions.text: Uses watch() with a getter: watch(() => route.params.id, cb)
Does NOT use watchEffect() (project convention)
May suggest { immediate: true } to also fetch on initial load
Uses useRoute() from vue-router to access route

evals.name: define-props-emits
evals.prompt: I'm creating a reusable input component that receives a value prop and emits changes. Show me the correct pattern.

assertions.text: Uses defineProps() with runtime declaration (type, required/default)
Uses defineEmits(['update:modelValue']) for v-model support
Uses <script setup> syntax exclusively
May mention defineModel() as Vue 3.4+ alternative

evals.name: template-ref-pattern
evals.prompt: How do I programmatically focus an input element after a button click in Vue 3?

assertions.text: Declares const inputEl = ref(null) for the template ref
Uses ref="inputEl" attribute in the template
Accesses via inputEl.value.focus() (with .value)
May wrap in nextTick() if focus depends on a state change

evals.name: computed-usage
evals.prompt: I have a list of items and want to show only active ones, plus display a count. What's the best pattern?

assertions.text: Uses computed() for the filtered list (derived state)
Uses computed() for the count (derived from filtered list)
Does NOT use a watcher to maintain a separate filtered array
Shows ref() for the source items array
